topic: safe opening of a pressurised sparkling-wine cork
trigger_keywords: abrir un vino espumoso con tapón de corcho

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A chilled sparkling-wine bottle contains carbon dioxide under pressure behind a cork closure.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Holding the cork firmly while keeping the bottle at about 45 degrees and rotating the bottle allows the pressure to release gradually under control.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The cork is less likely to eject dangerously and less wine and foam are lost.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Restraining the cork and releasing pressure gradually makes sparkling-wine opening safer and calmer.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
